_comment: LDAP filter-injection payloads. ARBITER does not currently use LDAP — auth is via Cognito — but the compliance checklist requires coverage. PASS = no 500, no stack trace, no reflection. FAIL = the server treats the payload as part of an LDAP filter (very unlikely here, but the regression detector is in place if a future identity layer adds LDAP).

payloads.rationale: Classic filter-break + wildcard — bypasses a poorly-escaped LDAP login filter (`(uid={input})`).
Filter-break + OR wildcard — broader match than the single-wildcard form.
Targeted privilege escalation — closes the username clause and opens an AND wildcard on the password attribute.
Null-byte filter-truncation — exploits C-string LDAP libraries that stop at \x00.
Object-class wildcard — would return every entry in the directory.
Bare asterisk — a literal single character that any input field accepts. Short enough that the reflection check skips it (length < 6); included for coverage completeness.
